destinations.evidence: Block verification gates required SPC attachments through the shared allowlist predicate before enforcing proof presence and signature verification.
The allowlist is centralized in precompile metadata, preventing verifier-local address drift.

equivalence_argument: The verifier uses the shared metadata predicate for both settlement and delivery SPC attachment eligibility, while malformed allowlisted proofs still reject through proof length/signature checks.
notes: Non-allowlisted attachments are not treated as satisfying required SPC evidence.
rationale: The commit replaces hand-written address comparisons in both extraction paths with the shared predicate and documents ignore-versus-hard-error semantics. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Block verification extracts and verifies `spc_calls` and `delivery_spc_calls` only for precompiles accepted by `is_spc_block_verified_precompile`; non-allowlisted attachments are ignored, while malformed allowlisted attachments remain block-invalid.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create transactions with `spc_calls` for an allowlisted precompile and a non-allowlisted precompile; assert the former is extracted and malformed proof rejects the block, while the latter is ignored.
summary: Block verifier uses the shared SPC allowlist.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create allowlisted and non-allowlisted SPC attachments and assert only allowlisted malformed attachments can make the block invalid.
title: Block verifier uses shared SPC allowlist
